source_document: New York S7543 (Automated Employment Decision Tools)
passage_text: Any employer or employment agency that uses an automated employment decision tool to screen a candidate or employee for an employment decision is to notify each such candidate or employee who resides in the city that an automated employment decision tool will be used in connection with the assessment or evaluation of such candidate or employee. Such notice is to be made no later than ten business days before such use and allow the candidate or employee to request an alternative selection process or accommodation.

obligation.subject: Any employer or employment agency that uses an automated employment decision tool
obligation.action: notify each candidate or employee who resides in the city that an automated employment decision tool will be used and allow request for alternative selection process or accommodation
obligation.condition: when using an automated employment decision tool to screen for an employment decision

timeline.compliance_deadline: no later than ten business days before such use
timeline.timeline_text: no later than ten business days before such use

ambiguity.interpretation_notes: The requirement to 'allow the candidate or employee to request an alternative selection process or accommodation' does not specify what constitutes an adequate alternative, whether the employer must grant the request, or what accommodations are sufficient.
ambiguity.suggested_clarification: Define minimum standards for alternative selection processes and specify whether employers have discretion to deny accommodation requests

rights_protection.right_description: Right to be notified at least ten business days before an automated employment decision tool is used for screening
rights_protection.trigger_condition: when employer uses AEDT for employment screening

expected_extractions.notes: Tests 'is to notify' and 'is to be made' obligation phrasing — neither uses shall/must/required. Previous keyword screening would miss this entirely.
